description:
## Goal

Hand-rolled renderer that turns a `Diagnostic` into the output sketched in the .20 epic:

```
warning[type/unreachable-arm]: unreachable branch
  --> fixtures/modules.fz:12:7
   |
12 |   if x == "hello", do: ...
   |      ^^^^^^^^^^^^ this can never be true
   |
   = note: `x` has type `int | atom(:ok)` here
   = note: `x == "hello"` would require `x : str`, but `(int | atom(:ok)) & str` is uninhabited
   = help: did you mean `x == :hello`?
```

## Dependencies

- Requires .20.1 (`SourceMap::locate`, `Span`).
- Requires .20.5 (`Diagnostic` type).
- Does NOT require .20.4 â€” the renderer takes `Diagnostic + SourceMap` and produces text. It's stage-agnostic.

## Why hand-roll

Surveyed: `ariadne` (lovely, but pulls `unicode-width`, `yansi`, `concolor`), `codespan-reporting` (more conservative deps), miette (heavy). Reasons to roll our own (~300 LOC):

1. **Custom shape.** Macro lineage display, set-theoretic type rendering, and future fz-specific affordances (e.g. process pid + frame for runtime errors) don't map cleanly to any library's data model.
2. **Lean deps.** Cargo.toml has 8 entries (mostly Cranelift). Adding 5+ transitive deps for diagnostic rendering would dominate compile time of the rest of the crate.
3. **Control over output.** Snapshot tests against the renderer are far simpler when the format is ours.

The rendered format follows rustc's general shape â€” a well-trodden idiom users already recognize. We're not innovating on the format; we're just owning it.

## Module

`src/diag/render.rs`.

## API

```rust
pub struct Renderer<'a> {
    pub sm: &'a SourceMap,
    pub color: ColorMode,
    pub tab_width: u32,           // default 4
    pub ascii_only: bool,         // v1 = true; v2 may switch carets to unicode
}

pub enum ColorMode { Auto, Always, Never }

impl<'a> Renderer<'a> {
    pub fn new(sm: &'a SourceMap) -> Self;
    pub fn emit(&self, d: &Diagnostic, out: &mut impl std::io::Write) -> std::io::Result<()>;
    pub fn emit_all(&self, diags: &Diagnostics, out: &mut impl std::io::Write) -> std::io::Result<()>;
}
```

`ColorMode::Auto`: enable color if `out` is a TTY (use `std::io::IsTerminal`, stable in Rust 1.70+) AND `NO_COLOR` is unset (https://no-color.org). Cargo.toml is on `edition = "2024"`, so the stable `IsTerminal` is available.

## Rendering algorithm

For a single `Diagnostic d`:

1. **Header line.**
```
<severity>[<code.0>]: <message>
```
Severity colors (when on): Error = bold red, Warning = bold yellow, Note = bold cyan, Help = bold green. The code is dim. Width-cap the line; if message is long, soft-wrap at terminal width (or 100 chars when no TTY).

2. **Primary location.**
```
  --> <file_name>:<line>:<col>
```
Computed via `sm.locate(d.primary.span)`. If `primary.span.is_dummy()`, print `  --> <generated>` and skip the snippet entirely.

3. **Source snippet.**
- Compute the line range covering the primary span (it may be multi-line).
- For each line in range:
  - Render a gutter: `{line:>width} | {source_line}`. `width` is determined by the largest line number across all spans in the diagnostic, minimum 2.
  - Re-expand tabs to `tab_width` spaces *for both the source line and the underline*. The renderer is the *only* place tabs get expanded.
- After each source line carrying span content, render an underline line:
  - `{spaces} | {leading_spaces}{carets}`
  - Primary span uses `^` (red, bold). Secondary spans use `-` (cyan or dim).
  - If a span fits on one line, carets span `[start_col, end_col)`. If multi-line, the first line gets `^^^^^...` to end-of-line, intermediate lines are skipped with a `...` continuation marker, last line gets a `...^^^^` prefix-only underline.
- Append the span's `label` after the carets (if any), separated by a space.

4. **Secondary spans.** Same treatment as primary, but `-` underline, less prominent color. Drawn after the primary snippet, each with its own `--> file:line:col` header *unless* it's in the same file and within ~5 lines of the primary (in which case fold into the same snippet block â€” rustc-style).

5. **Notes and helps.**
```
   = note: <text>
   = help: <text>
```
Each line wraps at column width.

6. **Macro lineage trailer.**
If `d.primary` has `SpanOrigin::Expanded { macro_call, definition }` (look up via the AST â€” but `Diagnostic` carries the span, not the origin â€” so this either requires the `expanded_from: Vec<Span>` field on `Diagnostic` from .20.5 to be populated by the *producer*, or the producer must walk the AST to find the origin).

**Decision:** the *producer* of the diagnostic (the stage code, called from .20.7) is responsible for populating `Diagnostic.expanded_from` by reading the offending node's `SpanOrigin`. The renderer just iterates `expanded_from`:

```
   = expanded from `<macro>` at <file>:<line>:<col>
```

One line per entry, in order from innermost expansion to outermost.

7. **Blank line between consecutive diagnostics** when `emit_all` is used.

## Tab handling

Source bytes contain raw `\t`. The renderer expands each `\t` to `tab_width` spaces *at render time*. Underline columns are computed in the expanded coordinate space.

Implementation:
- Read the source line bytes via `SourceMap`.
- Walk byte-by-byte; for each char, compute its *display width*:
  - `\t` â†’ `tab_width - (display_col % tab_width)` (round up to next tab stop)
  - ASCII printable â†’ 1
  - Non-ASCII (v1): treat as 1; flag as known-wrong (v2 uses `unicode-width`)
- Emit the expanded line into a `String`, and emit underline using the same width.

## Unicode (v1 scope)

ASCII-only carets (`^`, `-`, `|`, `=`). Unicode prettiness (`â•­â”€`, `â”‚`, `â•°â”€`) belongs in a future ticket â€” fz-specific UX work. The character set is parametrized so a v2 ticket flips a flag.

## Color implementation

No `crossterm` / `termcolor` dep. Use raw ANSI escapes via a small `style.rs` helper:

```rust
const RED_BOLD: &str = "\x1b[1;31m";
const RESET: &str = "\x1b[0m";
// ...
```

Wrap output strings with the escapes only when `color == ColorMode::Always` or (`Auto` && `is_tty()` && `NO_COLOR` unset).

## Tests

Snapshot tests are essential here. Use `insta` (or hand-rolled â€” pick one and stick with it across the .20 arc).

- **Single-span error**: synthesize a Diagnostic, render, snapshot the output.
- **Multi-line span**: span covers lines 12-14 of a 20-line file.
- **Adjacent secondary**: primary and secondary in same file, 3 lines apart â†’ folded into one snippet block.
- **Distant secondary**: primary and secondary 50 lines apart â†’ two snippet blocks with their own `-->` headers.
- **Macro lineage**: a `Diagnostic` with two `expanded_from` entries renders both trailer lines.
- **Tab in source**: line `\tlet x = 1` with caret on `x` â†’ caret aligned at column 5 (assuming tab_width=4).
- **DUMMY primary span**: prints `--> <generated>`, no snippet, notes still render.
- **Color off**: NO_COLOR set, output is plain text (golden file matches without escapes).
- **Color on**: ColorMode::Always, escapes appear at expected positions.

## Out of scope

- Suggested-edit rendering (text helps only).
- Line wrapping for very long source lines (truncate with `â€¦` indicator â€” implement basic version v1; pretty version later).
- Width-aware unicode (v2).
- Terminal hyperlinks (OSC 8) for `--> file:line:col` (v2).

## Deliverables

- `src/diag/render.rs` with `Renderer`.
- `src/diag/style.rs` with ANSI escape constants and tty detection.
- Snapshot test suite covering the cases above.
- Documentation block at the top of `render.rs` describing the format with one canonical example.
title: Renderer: snippets, carets, gutter, multi-span, lineage
